finding: Folk intuitions about an agent's judgment attribution differ when the agent lacks motivation; a majority attribute judgment when ability is shown separately, consistent with internalist conceptions.
Therapist monitoring behaviors and non-avoidance of feedback were associated with client-rated alliance and session outcomes in multilevel models.
Two information sources (newspaper agenda at time 1, televised advertising at time 1) predicted television news agenda at time 2, demonstrating bidirectional intermedia influence.

Both object types showed holistic representations in which complete stimuli were preferred over parts in recognition and matching tasks; this advantage was mandatory and could not be eliminated by cueing.
Moral considerations bias folk ascriptions of intentional action; immoral actions are more readily attributed intentionality, which may compromise juror impartiality in criminal cases.
Transparent morphological relations between words modulated parallel processing in a flanker task; suffix-flanked targets showed facilitation beyond orthographic effects.
Response scale range influenced behavioral reporting and subsequent judgments; respondents inferred typical behavior from response alternatives and used it as a comparison standard.
Assimilation and contrast effects in part-whole question sequences depend on perceived conversational context; nonredundancy norms shift interpretation when questions belong together.

Commercial interruptions disrupted hedonic adaptation and increased program enjoyment despite consumer preference for uninterrupted viewing; this effect required adaptation-prone programs and viewers.
Consumer affluence was the largest contributor to deindustrialization, followed by productivity growth and trade; global trade exerted both direct and indirect effects on manufacturing employment.
Wage gaps attributable to firm size increase with job responsibility, consistent with hierarchical matching where managerial talent amplification depends on subordinate scope.
Variety in an assortment reduced perceived quantity; identical items consolidated into a larger Gestalt, affecting both quantity estimates and consumption behavior.
Source certainty effects on persuasion depend on expertise; low-expertise sources gain influence when expressing certainty, whereas high-expertise sources gain influence when expressing uncertainty.

System-threatened individuals differ in system-defense strategy based on confidence in legitimacy; low-confidence individuals prefer indirect defense consumption, high-confidence individuals prefer direct defense.
Visual product orientation facilitating embodied mental simulation increased purchase intentions; occupying perceptual resources for motor simulation attenuated this effect.
Horizontal positioning of product images congruent with readers' spatial time representation (past-left, future-right) increased product attitudes; processing fluency mediated this effect.
Sellers construe products at higher levels than buyers; seller-buyer price discrepancy emerges when primary aspects superior, and differential construal mediates this gap for high-level features.
Resource constraint triggered two coping strategies: efficiency planning and priority planning; prioritization increased with constraint and was more prevalent among budgeters.
The mere presence of one's own smartphone reduced available cognitive capacity for other tasks, independent of actual phone use; the effect was strongest for smartphone-dependent individuals.
A one standard deviation decrease in median age increased new business formation by approximately 40% of mean rate; older age composition reduced entrepreneurship at every age.
